evals.prompt: I want to create a research repository for tracking AI for Mental Health papers. The focus should be on multi-turn dialogue systems for mental health support. I need help setting up the directory structure, configuration files, and domain-specific skills.
evals.expected_output: A complete research repository structure with: directory layout created (your-domain-repo/, .claude/skills/, paper_notes/), core configuration files customized (CLAUDE.md, research-focus.md, classification-guide.md, README.md, .gitignore, LICENSE), and two domain-specific skills (search-papers and add-paper) created with proper templates.

assertions.name: core_files_created
assertions.text: Core configuration files exist: CLAUDE.md, research-focus.md, classification-guide.md, .gitignore, LICENSE

assertions.name: claude_md_customized
assertions.text: CLAUDE.md is customized for AI for Mental Health domain with specific repository purpose

assertions.name: priorities_defined
assertions.text: research-focus.md defines 3 priority levels specific to mental health dialogue systems

assertions.name: tags_defined
assertions.text: classification-guide.md defines 3-4 tag categories with 8-15 total tags

evals.prompt: Help me migrate my existing literature collection on LLM for Education to an AI-assisted research workflow. I want to set up standardized tagging, automated search, and paper tracking with git-based version control.
evals.expected_output: Repository structure with focus on LLM for Education domain, including: 3-tier priority system (e.g., Personalized Learning, Assessment, Content Generation), 8-15 tags across 3-4 categories with color-coded badges, search-papers skill with 30-50 queries distributed across priorities, add-paper skill with standardized workflow, and proper git workflow configuration.

assertions.name: core_files_created
assertions.text: Core configuration files exist: CLAUDE.md, research-focus.md, classification-guide.md, .gitignore, LICENSE

assertions.name: priorities_llm_specific
assertions.text: research-focus.md defines LLM for Education-specific priorities (e.g., Educational Applications, LLM Techniques, Foundational Work)

assertions.name: search_queries_provided
assertions.text: research-focus.md contains 30-50 search queries distributed across priorities

assertions.name: tags_with_colors
assertions.text: classification-guide.md defines tags with color-coded badge URLs

assertions.name: git_workflow_configured
assertions.text: CLAUDE.md specifies git workflow (commit only README files, proper commit message format)

evals.prompt: I need to start tracking quantum computing research papers. Focus areas: quantum algorithms (highest priority), quantum hardware construction, and quantum applications. Set up the complete repository with bilingual support (English and Chinese).
evals.expected_output: Bilingual research repository (README.md and README_zh.md) with quantum-computing-specific configuration: Priority 1 (Quantum Algorithms), Priority 2 (Quantum Hardware), Priority 3 (Quantum Applications), complete tag system (e.g., Data: Benchmark/Simulation/Real_World, Method: VQA/QAOA/Optimization, Application: Chemistry/Finance/ML), and both search-papers and add-paper skills created.

assertions.name: bilingual_readme
assertions.text: Both README.md (English) and README_zh.md (Chinese) exist with translated content

assertions.name: quantum_priorities
assertions.text: research-focus.md defines quantum-specific priorities: Algorithms (P1), Hardware (P2), Applications (P3)

assertions.name: quantum_tags
assertions.text: classification-guide.md defines quantum-specific tags (e.g., VQA, QAOA, Error_Correction, Fault_Tolerant)

assertions.name: readme_tag_badges
assertions.text: README files contain badge URLs for tags with proper color coding
